turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been having some pretty bad abdominal pain and bloody stools for the past 3 weeks. The pain is crampy and mostly on my left side, and I've had bright red blood mixed with stool. It's really been bothering me, especially since it's gotten worse over time.

turns.student: Can you describe the main symptom you're experiencing?
turns.patient: The main symptom that's bringing me in today is definitely the abdominal pain, which feels like crampy spasms on my left side. It's constant and doesn't seem to be relieved by anything, not even after I've had a bowel movement. The bloody stools are also really concerning for me - it's bright red blood mixed with stool, and it's happening way more frequently than usual.

turns.student: When did this start?
turns.patient: It started about 3 weeks ago, but to be honest, I've been feeling a bit off for even longer. The pain has just gotten progressively worse over time, and the bloody stools have become more frequent too. It's really affecting my work and daily life, which is why I'm here seeking help today.

turns.student: How often does it happen?
turns.patient: The crampy abdominal pain on my left side has been constant for about 3 weeks now. The bloody stools are also a new development, happening with almost every bowel movement - it's like 8-10 times a day, which is way more than usual. I used to have a pretty regular bowel routine, but this has completely disrupted that.

turns.student: How severe is it on a scale of 1 to 10?
turns.patient: I'd say it's an 8 or 9 out of 10 in terms of severity - the pain is constant and doesn't really let up, and the bloody stools are just really scary for me. It's not just the physical discomfort, but also the worry that something serious might be going on.

turns.student: Do you notice any other symptoms when it happens?
turns.patient: The increased frequency of bowel movements has been a big issue - I'm talking 8-10 times a day. The stools are loose to watery, and I feel this urgent need to go right away. It's really disrupted my daily routine and work performance. Also, I've noticed that the diarrhea is getting worse with time, especially since I started taking loperamide (Imodium) for it - it's given me some minimal relief, but not enough to make a significant difference.

turns.student: Does it get worse with any particular activity?
turns.patient: To be honest, the pain doesn't really seem to worsen or improve with any specific activity. It's just constant and ongoing. But I have noticed that my fatigue has been getting worse, especially since I've had to deal with this diarrhea nonstop. It's like I'm running on empty all the time, which is making it hard for me to keep up with work.

turns.student: Do you have any sweating, nausea, or dizziness with it?
turns.patient: No, I don't experience any of those symptoms when I have the abdominal pain and bloody stools. The only thing that's really affecting me is the constant fatigue and weakness, which makes it hard for me to keep up with my daily activities and work.

turns.student: Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
turns.patient: The pain is mostly confined to my lower abdomen on the left side. Occasionally, I might feel a bit of achy pain in both knees and ankles, but it's not directly related to the abdominal pain or bloody stools. Those symptoms seem more like joint issues, which have been going on for a while now.

turns.student: Do you have any swelling in your legs or feet?
turns.patient: No, I don't have any swelling in my legs or feet. The achy pain in my knees and ankles is just general stiffness that's been ongoing for a bit longer than the abdominal issues. It's not really related to what's going on with my gut, from what I can tell.

turns.student: What medications are you currently taking?
turns.patient: I'm taking loperamide (Imodium) for the diarrhea, but it's only given me minimal relief. Otherwise, I don't take any other medications regularly. However, I do use ibuprofen 2-3 times a week for the joint pain in my knees and ankles, which seems to worsen the abdominal symptoms when I take it.

turns.student: Do you have any medical conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes?
turns.patient: No, I don't have any known medical conditions. My father has type 2 diabetes, but my mother is healthy and I'm not aware of anyone else in my family with significant health issues. I've always been relatively healthy, except for that one appendectomy at age 16.

turns.student: Have you ever had your cholesterol checked?
turns.patient: No, I don't think so. To be honest, I haven't seen a doctor in over two years, and my last bloodwork or tests were probably done around that time. I've been pretty regular with my check-ups before, but things have just gotten busy lately with work and other responsibilities.

turns.student: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
turns.patient:
I don't smoke at all, but I do drink a couple of beers most weeknights to help me relax after a long day. However, over the past month, my consumption has increased to 4-5 beers per night due to stress from work and dealing with these symptoms. 

Also, I should mention that I've been feeling pretty stressed out about all this, especially since it's affecting my work and daily life. My job as a software engineer can be demanding, but usually, I'm able to manage my stress levels okay. However, lately, everything just feels overwhelming, and I feel like I'm barely keeping up.

turns.student: How would you describe your diet and exercise habits?
turns.patient:
To be honest, my diet has been pretty irregular due to my long work hours and high stress levels. I've been eating a lot of takeout and fast food, which isn't great for my gut, I'm sure. As for exercise, I try to stay active by going to the gym 2-3 times a week, but it's not as consistent as I'd like. Lately, with everything that's been going on, I've barely had the energy to get out of bed, let alone go to the gym.

I also want to mention that my sister has Crohn's disease, which might be relevant given my symptoms. But other than that, there aren't any significant family medical history or genetic factors that I'm aware of.

And just to recap, the main thing that's been bothering me is this constant abdominal pain and bloody stools. The pain is crampy and mostly on my left side, and it's getting worse over time. The bloody stools are also really concerning for me - they're bright red blood mixed with stool, and I'm having them way more frequently than usual.

I've tried taking loperamide (Imodium) to help with the diarrhea, but it's only given me minimal relief. And like I said earlier, my joint pain in my knees and ankles seems to worsen when I take ibuprofen for it. That's about it - just trying to get some answers and find a way to manage these symptoms!

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had heart disease or heart attacks?
turns.patient: No, I don't think so. My father has type 2 diabetes, but I'm not aware of any history of heart disease or heart attacks in my immediate family.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had other serious medical conditions?
turns.patient: My mother is healthy and doesn't have a history of any significant health issues. Like I mentioned earlier, my sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease at age 30, but that's about it for our immediate family's medical history.

turns.student: When was the last time you saw a doctor?
turns.patient: I haven't seen a doctor in over two years. Like I said earlier, my last bloodwork or tests were probably done around that time. To be honest, things have just gotten really busy with work and other responsibilities, so I haven't prioritized seeing a doctor until now when these symptoms started getting worse.

turns.student: Do you have any allergies to medications?
turns.patient: I don't have any known drug allergies that I'm aware of.

turns.student: Is there anything else you think I should know about your health?
turns.patient: I've been trying to manage my stress levels with exercise and meditation, but it's been really tough lately. The diarrhea has been waking me up at night 2-3 times a week, which is also affecting my sleep quality. It's like I'm constantly feeling exhausted and run down.
